content.problemStatement:
Chef has a string SSS with length NNN and integer KKK. Let's consider all strings that can be obtained by erasing KKK consecutive characters from the original string ‚Äï specifically, for each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N‚àíK+11 \le i \le N-K+11‚â§i‚â§N‚àíK+1), let f(i)f(i)f(i) be the string that is obtained by erasing the characters at positions i,i+1,‚Ä¶,i+K‚àí1i, i+1, \ldots, i + K - 1i,i+1,‚Ä¶,i+K‚àí1.

Consider a permutation PPP of the integers 111 through N‚àíK+1N - K + 1N‚àíK+1. You need to find the lexicographically smallest permutation PPP such that f(Pi)‚â§f(Pi+1)f(P_i) \leq f(P_{i+1})f(Pi‚Äã)‚â§f(Pi+1‚Äã) for each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N‚àíK1 \le i \le N - K1‚â§i‚â§N‚àíK).

Since the length of this permutation can be too large, you should only compute its hash: ‚àëi=1N‚àíK+1Pi‚ãÖi\sum_{i=1}^{N-K+1} P_i \cdot i‚àëi=1N‚àíK+1‚ÄãPi‚Äã‚ãÖi modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10710^7107
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains a single string SSS with length NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the hash of the lexicographically smallest valid permutation.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: f(1)f(1)f(1) and f(2)f(2)f(2) are both "a", the lexicographically smallest permutation is P=[1,2]P = [1, 2]P=[1,2]. The answer is 1‚ãÖ1+2‚ãÖ2=41 \cdot 1 + 2 \cdot 2 = 41‚ãÖ1+2‚ãÖ2=4.
Example case 2: f(1)f(1)f(1) is "cde", f(2)f(2)f(2) is "ade", f(3)f(3)f(3) is "abe", f(4)f(4)f(4) is "abc". The lexicographically smallest valid permutation is P=[4,3,2,1]P = [4, 3, 2, 1]P=[4,3,2,1] and the answer is 4‚ãÖ1+3‚ãÖ2+2‚ãÖ3+1‚ãÖ4=204 \cdot 1 + 3 \cdot 2 + 2 \cdot 3 + 1 \cdot 4 = 204‚ãÖ1+3‚ãÖ2+2‚ãÖ3+1‚ãÖ4=20.
